name: Chrismas Tree Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream together the margarine/butter and sugar until smooth.
2. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Stir in vanilla.
3. Gradually add the self-raising flour mixture to the butter mixture, mixing until just combined.
4. Form 2-3 inch balls of dough with your hands.
5. Flatten the balls slightly and place them on a cookie sheet, arranging about 6 per sheet.
6. Bake at 375°F (190°C) for about 15 minutes or until lightly golden brown.
7. Frost the cooled cookies using icing made from 1 lb confectioner's sugar mixed with 4 tbsp meringue powder and icing paste (both can be found in cake decorating sections of stores). Make small holes in the cookies while still warm, then open them to allow the frosting to soak in.
Serving Ideas: These cookies are sturdy enough to be used as ornaments.
